title: Future Continuous and Future Perfect
description: Master two advanced future tenses: will be + -ing for actions in progress at a specific future moment, will have + past participle for actions completed by a future point, the future perfect continuous, and precise contrasts with will and going to.
sources: CEFR B2 descriptors (Council of Europe, Common European Framework of Reference)
Raymond Murphy, English Grammar in Use (future continuous; future perfect; will and going to)
